description_long: This bungalow is a single-story residential building with an elongated, rectangular footprint and several protruding sections, likely representing rooms or extensions. The roof appears mostly flat with some slight elevation changes, and the exterior walls are finished in a light color, possibly stucco or painted concrete. Multiple windows and doorways are distributed along the facade, with some areas featuring larger window groupings, suggesting living spaces or common rooms. The structure includes a central main section with two wings extending perpendicularly, creating a T-shaped or cross-like layout. There is a visible porch or covered entry area at the front, providing a sheltered entrance. The overall design is practical and low-profile, typical of suburban or urban bungalows.
description: A single-story, light-colored bungalow with a flat roof, multiple window groupings, and a central porch, featuring a T-shaped layout with extended wings.
description_view_0: This view shows the bungalow's elongated main section with a prominent central porch and several windowed extensions on both sides.
description_view_1: From this angle, the perpendicular wings and the depth of the extensions are more visible, highlighting the building's T-shaped layout.
description_view_2: This side view emphasizes the full length of the bungalow, showing the linear arrangement of rooms and the flat roof profile.
description_view_3: The rear perspective reveals additional extensions and the arrangement of back-facing windows, as well as the overall compactness of the structure.
